input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue with keyboard handling in .NET 10. This is a known change — `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed because Android itself deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`. In .NET 10 (and newer Android), the recommended approach is to use the `Edge-to-Edge` display and `WindowInsets` / `Keyboard` padding.

Let me provide a concise, helpful answer based on my knowledge of .NET MAUI and Android keyboard handling changes.

In .NET 9+, Android deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`. In .NET 10 MAUI, the recommended approach is to use the new keyboard insets handling. Specifically:


Android deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` back in API 30, and MAUI has been moving away from it. The modern solution involves using `Edge2Edge` with `WindowInsets` APIs, or leveraging MAUI's built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` for automatic entry/editor scrolling, or manually handling insets with `Window.SetDecorFitsSystemWindows(false)` for custom keyboard behavior.

For .NET 10 specifically, `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` won't work reliably anymore due to Android's deprecation, so the better approach is using `SoftInputMode = Adjust.Pan` which pans the window up without resizing, paired with proper layout configuration. Setting up the insets listener to handle keyboard visibility dynamically, which is the modern approach in .NET 10 since `AdjustResize` is deprecated. The framework now uses edge-to-edge rendering by default, so I need to work with window insets rather than relying on the old soft input mode behavior. Writing the keyboard insets handler...
